reasoning: The US has consistently avoided deploying conventional ground forces to Yemen, relying instead on air, naval, and special operations assets (which are excluded by the resolution criteria). There is no political or strategic appetite for placing regular troops on the ground, as a conventional campaign against the Houthis would be costly and highly risky. While a crashed aircraft, rescue mission, or temporary logistical visit by regular forces remains within the realm of possibility, these edge cases are very improbable. Therefore, a very low probability, aligned closely with the market freeze value of roughly 5.65%, is justified.
